---
title: "Research"
subtitle: "Research lines, ongoing projects, and open-source software"
---

::: {.snuc-org-hero .compact}
<div class="snuc-org-hero-content">
<div class="snuc-eyebrow">What We Do</div>
<h1>Research</h1>
<p class="lead">Research lines, funded projects, and open-source software advancing the theory and methods of social network science.</p>
</div>
:::

## Research Areas

<div class="snuc-area-head"><span class="ac-letter">A</span><span class="ac-title">Theory &amp; Methods</span><span class="ac-tag">How we study networks</span></div>

::: {.snuc-cards .areas-desc}
<div class="snuc-card"><h4>Social Network Analysis &amp; Network Science</h4><p>Foundational concepts, measures, and models for studying relational structure across the social and behavioral sciences.</p></div>
<div class="snuc-card"><h4>Statistical Network Models</h4><p>ERGMs, stochastic actor-oriented models (SAOMs), and relational event models for inference on network structure and change.</p></div>
<div class="snuc-card"><h4>Mathematical Sociology</h4><p>Formal and generative models that link micro-level mechanisms to macro-level social structure.</p></div>
:::

<div class="snuc-area-head"><span class="ac-letter">B</span><span class="ac-title">Computation, Data &amp; Software</span><span class="ac-tag">The tools we build and use</span></div>

::: {.snuc-cards .areas-desc}
<div class="snuc-card"><h4>Computational Social Science</h4><p>Simulation- and data-intensive approaches that combine computation with social theory.</p></div>
<div class="snuc-card"><h4>Network Data Science &amp; Graph Machine Learning</h4><p>Learning representations, patterns, and predictions from graph-structured data.</p></div>
<div class="snuc-card"><h4>Methods &amp; Open-Source Software</h4><p>Reproducible tools for network analysis, including the R package <em>netmem</em>.</p></div>
:::

<div class="snuc-area-head"><span class="ac-letter">C</span><span class="ac-title">Substantive Domains</span><span class="ac-tag">Where we apply networks</span></div>

::: {.snuc-cards .areas-desc}
<div class="snuc-card"><h4>Science of Science &amp; Knowledge</h4><p>How researchers, ideas, and institutions form networks that shape the production of scientific knowledge.</p></div>
<div class="snuc-card"><h4>Governance &amp; Policy Networks</h4><p>Inter-organizational and inter-municipal coordination in public administration and metropolitan governance.</p></div>
<div class="snuc-card"><h4>Organizational Networks</h4><p>Informal structure, collaboration, and influence within and between organizations.</p></div>
<div class="snuc-card"><h4>Educational Networks</h4><p>Relational processes in schools and educational systems, from equity to the diffusion of innovations.</p></div>
:::

## Projects

::: {.snuc-projects}
<div class="snuc-project">
<div class="proj-meta"><span class="grant">ANID/FONDECYT Nº3250037 · 2025–2027</span><h3>Group Dynamics</h3></div>
<div class="proj-body">
<p class="desc">Understanding how social networks and individual characteristics co-evolve within group contexts.</p>
<p class="investigators">Principal Investigator: Alejandro Espinosa-Rada.</p>
</div>
</div>

<div class="snuc-project">
<div class="proj-meta"><span class="grant">ANID/FONDECYT Nº1261889 · 2026–2028</span><h3>New Approaches to the Study of Social Mobility</h3></div>
<div class="proj-body">
<p class="desc">Expanding theory, data, and methods for the study of social mobility.</p>
<p class="investigators">Principal Investigator: Mauricio Bucca. Co-Principal Investigators: Xi Song, Alejandro Espinosa-Rada.</p>
</div>
</div>

<div class="snuc-project">
<div class="proj-meta"><span class="grant">ANID/FONDECYT Nº1261268 · 2026–2028</span><h3>Metropolitan Governance</h3></div>
<div class="proj-body">
<p class="desc">The institutional complexity of metropolitan governance networks in Chile and Colombia.</p>
<p class="investigators">Principal Investigator: Karina Arias Yurisch. Co-Principal Investigators: Karina Retamal, Alejandro Espinosa-Rada, Cristian Leyton.</p>
</div>
</div>

<div class="snuc-project">
<div class="proj-meta"><span class="grant">Concurso Avanza UC 2025 · 2026–2027</span><h3>Governing Complexity</h3></div>
<div class="proj-body">
<p class="desc">Applying multilevel network analysis to the institutional framework of metropolitan environmental governance — part of the group's broader SNA-SES (Social Networks and Socio-ecological Sustainability) research line, alongside the UC–UNSW Seed Fund (2025–2026, with Pavel Krivitsky) and prior ANID/FONDECYT Nº1220560 (2022–2025).</p>
<p class="investigators">Responsible Investigator: Karina Arias. Principal Investigator: Alejandro Espinosa-Rada.</p>
</div>
</div>
:::

## Software

::: {.snuc-card}
### netmem
An R package for handling and transforming network data across formats commonly used in social network analysis, developed and maintained within the group.

- [CRAN page](https://cran.r-project.org/web/packages/netmem/index.html)
- Install: `install.packages("netmem")`
:::

<!-- TODO: add further software tools/packages as they are released. -->
